CREATE TABLE TestTable (id INT, value INT);
SELECT * FROM TestTable WHERE value <= (SELECT MAX(value) FROM TestTable);
SELECT a.id, a.value FROM TestTable a JOIN TestTable b ON a.value <= b.value WHERE b.id = 2;
SELECT id FROM TestTable WHERE value <= (SELECT AVG(value) FROM TestTable) AND id <= 2;
CREATE TABLE AnotherTable (id INT, score INT);
SELECT * FROM AnotherTable WHERE score <= (SELECT MAX(score) FROM AnotherTable) AND score <= 20;
SELECT t1.id FROM TestTable t1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM AnotherTable t2 WHERE t1.value <= t2.score);
SELECT CASE WHEN value <= 15 THEN 'low' ELSE 'high' END as value_category FROM TestTable;
SELECT DISTINCT id FROM TestTable WHERE value <= (SELECT value FROM TestTable WHERE id = 1) ORDER BY id;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM TestTable WHERE value <= 10;
SELECT id FROM TestTable WHERE value <= (SELECT MIN(value) FROM TestTable WHERE id != 1) AND value <= 5;
